title: write-guard deny rule for direct .bee state edits
action: Add the deny rule in .bee/bin/lib/guards.mjs checkWrite (source: skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/guards.mjs — verify which is source of truth and edit source first): as the FIRST check, before phase logic and before GATE_ALLOWED_PREFIXES allowances, deny relPath .bee/state.json or .bee/backlog.jsonl in every phase. Denial message follows ERROR/WHY/FIX and names the replacement verbs (bee_state.mjs set/gate/worker/scribing-run; bee_backlog.mjs add). Validated facts to honor: the write-guard hook already routes Edit/Write/MultiEdit AND extracted Bash targets (redirection, sed -i, cp/mv/tee) through checkWrite — so cat >> .bee/backlog.jsonl is caught; a plain CLI invocation (node .bee/bin/bee_state.mjs set ...) extracts no target and passes; node -e escapes are mechanically unreachable — prose rule covers them (cell 3). Create hooks/test_write_guard.mjs following hooks/test_model_guard.mjs fixture style: deny row (Edit .bee/state.json → denied, message names CLI verb), bash-redirect row (cat >> .bee/backlog.jsonl → denied), pass rows (Edit .bee/cells/x.json passes; Bash node .bee/bin/bee_state.mjs set passes), fail-open row exercising the HOOK-level try/catch (fail-open is the hook wrapper property, not the pure checkWrite rule — e.g. corrupt state.json fixture proves the wrapper still exits 0). Sync the changed lib to .bee/bin/lib/guards.mjs (vendor copy identical).

truths: checkWrite denies .bee/state.json and .bee/backlog.jsonl in every phase, before allow-prefix logic, with ERROR/WHY/FIX naming the CLI verb
Bash redirection into the two files is denied; plain bee CLI invocations pass untouched
Paths outside the two targets keep exactly their current verdicts
The hook wrapper try/catch still fails open around the new rule (corrupt-state fixture exits 0)
Template lib and .bee/bin vendor copy are identical

artifacts.substantive: fixture test covering deny, bash-redirect deny, pass-through, and fail-open rows
first-hit deny rule for the two state files

key_links: Denial message verbs match the shipped CLI surface of cells 1-2
prohibitions: Do not weaken or remove any existing write-guard rule
Do not deny the bee CLIs' own writes (they are not tool calls)

trace.outcome: First-hit deny rule in checkWrite blocks direct Edit/Write/Bash-redirect edits to .bee/state.json and .bee/backlog.jsonl in every phase (before GATE_ALLOWED_PREFIXES/phase logic), naming bee_state.mjs/bee_backlog.mjs as the fix; hooks/test_write_guard.mjs (new, 20 assertions) covers deny/bash-redirect/pass-through/idle-precedence/fail-open rows; template and vendored guards.mjs kept byte-identical.
